summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard recording after the ultrabox shuts down, but the performance is judged only average.
review: The tape captures a pure soundboard source that becomes exceptionally clear once Healy's ultrabox cuts out during Minglewood Blues, earning high marks for audio fidelity. Reviewers note the show is decent yet not especially jam‑heavy, describing the playing as merely okay. Overall the recording shines while the musical performance receives mixed praise.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews rate the set 3‑4 stars, praising the soundboard quality highly but describing the playing as only average; combined weighting yields an overall ~4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Ultrabox malfunction adds noise until it shuts off early in Minglewood Blues; after that the sound is crisp, clear and well balanced with no other issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers did not comment on set pacing or song transitions; focus was on recording quality rather than musical flow.
